name: Git Utilities (VS Code extension)
description:
Async wrappers around the git CLI used by the VS Code extension package (NOT the Electron workbench app — that has its own git layer in app-exec-git / app-worktrees). Exports: gitRaw (with timeoutMs support), listWorktrees, addWorktree, removeWorktree, mergedWorktrees, countUncommitted, aheadBehind, findRepoRoot, findRepoKey, plus the Worktree interface. Re-exports buildRepoKey and repoNameFromCommonDir from @code-workbench/mcp-core/repo-key. `removeWorktree` delegates to @code-workbench/git-utils/remove-worktree for the robust removal flow (timeout + retry + force-detach + pending-deletion rescue).</description>
<parameter name="files">["packages/extension/src/git.ts"]
guidelines: gitRaw accepts an optional `timeoutMs` and surfaces timeouts as `Error('Timed out after Nms: git ...')` so the shared removeWorktreeRobust core recognises them and falls back to manual rm.

removeWorktree takes an optional PendingDeletionStore; the extension passes a globalState-backed store from extension.ts (PENDING_DELETIONS_KEY) so Windows-locked directories survive a window reload and are retried on next activation.
anti_patterns: Don't reintroduce the inline `git worktree remove --force; fs.rm` fallback — it lacks timeout, retry, detach-on-lock, and pending-deletion recovery. Always go through shared-git-utils.
decisions: Worktree-removal robustness lives in @code-workbench/git-utils so the Electron app and the VS Code extension share one implementation — see [[shared-git-utils]].
